---
title: "Programme"
subtitle: "15–19 June 2026, Lorentz Center, Leiden"
---

## Overview

Each day of the workshop builds systematically toward bridging methodological gaps between survival analysis and infectious disease modeling.

| Day | Theme | Goal |
|-----|-------|------|
| **Day 1** | Foundation Setting | Make disciplinary (dis)connections explicit |
| **Day 2** | Methodological Exploration | Create practical pathways for cross-disciplinary learning |
| **Day 3** | Integration & Refinement | Reveal realistic potential and boundaries of cross-disciplinary approaches |
| **Day 4** | Knowledge Transfer Tools | Produce concrete tools and guidance for the wider research community |
| **Day 5** | Consolidation & Future Directions | Ensure lasting impact and continued cross-disciplinary collaboration |

---

## Detailed Schedule

### Day 1 — Foundation Setting (Mon 15 June)

| Time | Activity |
|------|----------|
| 9:00–10:00 | Arrival and registration |
| 10:00–10:30 | Intro to workshop; welcome and opening by the Lorentz Center |
| 10:30–12:00 | Icebreakers · Introduction to Survival Analysis (lecture + Q&A) |
| 12:00–14:00 | Lunch |
| 14:00–15:30 | Introduction to Infectious Disease Modeling (lecture + Q&A) · Divide into mixed small groups; introduce Case Studies A and B; discuss initial analysis ideas |
| 15:30–16:00 | Afternoon break |
| 16:00–17:00 | **Plenary / Wrap-up:** Groups present first ideas on how to approach their case study |
| 17:00–19:00 | Welcome reception |

---

### Day 2 — Methodological Exploration (Tue 16 June)

| Time | Activity |
|------|----------|
| 9:00–10:00 | Small group work: explore data, develop analysis plans, begin implementation |
| 10:00–10:30 | Morning break |
| 10:30–12:00 | Continue analyses · Prepare preliminary findings |
| 12:00–12:30 | Keynote presentation by IDM key participant: case studies illustrating challenges and areas for collaboration |
| 12:30–14:00 | Lunch |
| 14:00–15:30 | Intergroup presentation and discussion: groups working on the same case study discuss and compare analysis plans · Groups separate and refine |
| 15:30–16:00 | Afternoon break |
| 16:00–17:00 | **Plenary / Wrap-up:** Discuss both case studies with everyone |
| 18:30 | Conference dinner |

---

### Day 3 — Integration & Refinement (Wed 17 June)

| Time | Activity |
|------|----------|
| 9:00–10:00 | Open discussion of ideas, challenges, and outstanding questions |
| 10:00–10:30 | Morning break |
| 10:30–12:00 | Refine case study analysis plan · Implement refined analysis plan |
| 12:00–14:00 | Lunch |
| 14:00–15:30 | Continue working on case studies in small groups; create code examples and prepare presentation of final analysis plan |
| 15:30–16:00 | Afternoon break |
| 16:00–17:00 | **Plenary / Wrap-up:** Group progress reports · Identify key learnings |

---

### Day 4 — Knowledge Transfer Tools (Thu 18 June)

| Time | Activity |
|------|----------|
| 9:00–10:00 | Introduction to "Ten Simple Rules" concept · Begin brainstorming rules · Form writing and coding teams |
| 10:00–10:30 | Morning break |
| 10:30–12:00 | Begin vignette development in mixed groups · Document code examples · Write Ten Simple Rules |
| 12:00–12:30 | Keynote presentation by SA key participant: case studies illustrating challenges and areas for collaboration |
| 12:30–14:00 | Lunch |
| 14:00–15:30 | Continue vignette development |
| 15:30–16:00 | Afternoon break |
| 16:00–17:00 | **Plenary / Wrap-up:** Discuss draft Ten Simple Rules |

---

### Day 5 — Consolidation & Future Directions (Fri 19 June)

| Time | Activity |
|------|----------|
| 9:00–10:00 | Plenary: Review and finalize Ten Simple Rules · Presentations of vignettes and code examples |
| 10:00–10:30 | Morning break |
| 10:30–12:00 | Future tasks and responsibilities · Finalize action plans · Assign post-workshop responsibilities · Closing activity / celebration |
| 12:00–14:00 | Lunch |
| 14:00–15:30 | Optional activities (outreach to broader audience) |
| 15:30–16:00 | Afternoon break |
| 16:00–17:00 | Optional plenary / wrap-up |

---

## Workshop Outcomes

By the end of the week, participants will have collectively produced:

1. **Vignettes** — worked examples demonstrating cross-disciplinary analytical approaches applied to the two case studies, with annotated code in R/Python
2. **Ten Simple Rules** — a document providing practical guidance for integrating SA and IDM methods, suitable for publication
3. **Online resource** — this website hosting the ten-rules document, code examples, and workshop materials for the wider research community
4. **Collaboration plans** — identified research gaps, assigned post-workshop responsibilities, and agreed next steps for continued cross-disciplinary work
